Let $a_n$ ($n \ge 1$) be the sequence of numbers defined by the recurrence relation
$$ a_1 = 1, \quad a_n = a_{n-1} a_1 + a_{n-2} a_2 + \dots + a_2 a_{n-2} + a_1 a_{n-1} $$
(so $a_2 = a_1^2 = 1$, $a_3 = 2a_1 a_2 = 2,$ etc.). Prove that
$\displaystyle{\sum_{n=1}^{\infty} \left(\frac{2}{9}\right)^n a_n = \frac{1}{3}}$.
